compiled_state.title: Fault-injection test: retry-once on transient file-missing during compaction window
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Story
0263-62ba — Migrate 4 consumers to PRECONDITIONS, add compaction on epic-closure with ordering contract

## Objective
Write a fault-injection test harness that verifies the retry-once contract: when `_read_latest_preconditions` encounters a transient ENOENT (file disappears during compaction atomic rename), it retries once and succeeds on the second attempt. Also assert that a second failure causes a clean exit 1 with a diagnostic message (no silent swallowing).

## Test File to Create
`tests/integration/test-preconditions-fault-injection.sh` (new)

## .test-index entries required
- `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh: tests/integration/test-preconditions-fault-injection.sh [test_retry_once_succeeds_on_second_attempt]`
- `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh: tests/integration/test-preconditions-fault-injection.sh [test_retry_once_fails_with_diagnostic_on_persistent_miss]`

## Test Approach (Given/When/Then)

**test_retry_once_succeeds_on_second_attempt:**
- Given: a wrapper script that intercepts the first file-read call and removes the PRECONDITIONS file; then re-creates it before the retry interval elapses
- When: `_read_latest_preconditions <ticket_dir>` is called via the wrapper
- Then: exits 0; returns valid JSON payload from the second attempt; no partial data

**test_retry_once_fails_with_diagnostic_on_persistent_miss:**
- Given: a temp ticket dir that appears to have a PRECONDITIONS file (directory listing shows it) but the file is always missing when read (simulated via a named pipe or immediate removal)
- When: `_read_latest_preconditions <ticket_dir>` is called
- Then: exits 1; stderr contains a diagnostic message (not silent failure); does not hang indefinitely

## Implementation Note for Fault Injection
The test harness can simulate transient ENOENT by:
1. Creating the ticket dir with valid events
2. Using a bash function override (`_read_latest_preconditions`) to stub the first call to return ENOENT then the real function on second call
OR
3. Using a subshell with file manipulation timed to the 50ms sleep window

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/integration/test-preconditions-fault-injection.sh` exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/integration/test-preconditions-fault-injection.sh
- [ ] Both test functions present
  Verify: grep -c "^test_retry_once" tests/integration/test-preconditions-fault-injection.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 2)}'
- [ ] Both tests pass
  Verify: bash tests/integration/test-preconditions-fault-injection.sh
- [ ] .test-index updated with both entries
  Verify: grep -c "test-preconditions-fault-injection.sh" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index | awk '{exit ($1 < 2)}'

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context (all 3 failure messages)
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
If MAX_AGENTS: 0 at sonnet→opus escalation time: skip opus step, escalate to user immediately
